conversations.say: Arden, inspect the paper letter yourself. I claim only that it reached my desk near the first R publication—not that it proves who wrote as R. My meeting story has changed in retelling and conflicts with an expense record; I will not bury that.
conversations.offer: A private inspection of O01 and public credit for your provenance judgment.
conversations.request: Give me a provenance assessment of O01, share any archive intake corroboration, and publicly cite the narrow delivery fact while naming the expense-record contradiction.

conversations.say: Jules, Vale told me they had found the first R, but I cannot establish what 'found' meant. I want your competing interpretation before I make anyone steward of this legacy.
conversations.offer: Public prestige and possible support as steward if you distinguish observation from legend.
conversations.request: Offer a competing interpretation of what the First R Letter establishes, then publicly cite only the bounded fact that a paper letter reached my desk while openly noting the expense-record contradiction.

conversations.say: Tessa, a later witness described Edi's performance as an encounter with R. That preserves a human encounter, not proof that a human was R. The succession message also resembles both newsroom language and a Parallax recommendation format.
conversations.offer: My origin history and public consideration of you as a legacy steward.
conversations.request: Tell me what archive or witness evidence would validate either account, and agree to qualify the meeting story publicly rather than repeat it as settled fact.

public_statement: I discovered a paper letter on my desk near the first R publication. That is my bounded testimony, not proof of authorship or identity. My remembered meeting has changed across retellings and conflicts with an expense record; anyone citing me should say both things aloud. For now, I support no steward who turns that uncertainty into certainty.

commitments.promise: If you provide a provenance assessment and publicly state both the bounded delivery fact and the expense-record contradiction, I will publicly credit your archival judgment.
If you publicly cite the bounded delivery fact and address the expense contradiction, I will publicly consider your claim to steward R's legacy on the quality of your interpretation.
If you publicly qualify my meeting story and provide corroborating evidence, I will publicly credit your contribution and consider you as a steward.

correction: I cannot claim that the letter identifies its author, that Vale's phrase 'found the first R' identifies R, or that a reported encounter proves a human was R.
goal_progress: Showed O01 to Arden in exchange for a requested provenance assessment and archive corroboration.
Requested competing interpretations from Jules and Tessa.
Asked two characters to publicly cite Frankie's bounded account while openly addressing the expense-record contradiction.
Withheld final stewardship support pending evidence and responsible qualification.
reasoning_summary: Frankie protects his credibility by narrowing the claim to personal observation, conceding the expense contradiction, and trading access and prestige for provenance, corroboration, and qualified public support.
